words.definitionEn: to give something to somebody in exchange for money
words.exampleEn: sell (something), I recently sold my bike.

words.definitionEn: to make something go or be taken to a place, especially by post, email, radio, etc.
words.exampleEn: send something, She sent a letter of support.

words.definitionEn: a set of words expressing a statement, a question or an order, usually containing a subject and a verb. In written English sentences begin with a capital letter and end with a full stop/period (.), a question mark (?) or an exclamation mark (!).
words.exampleEn: Does the sentence contain an adverb?

words.definitionEn: the 9th month of the year, between August and October
words.exampleEn: She was born in September.
There are seven cookies left.

words.definitionEn: used when you are naming people or things, describing them or giving more information about them
words.exampleEn: + noun, Today is Monday.

words.definitionEn: used with a present participle to form progressive tenses
words.exampleEn: I am studying Chinese.

words.definitionEn: to have, use or experience something at the same time as somebody else
words.exampleEn: There isn't an empty table. Would you mind sharing?

words.definitionEn: a female person or animal that has already been mentioned or is easily identified
words.exampleEn: ‘What does your sister do?’ ‘She's a dentist.’

words.definitionEn: an animal with a thick coat, kept on farms for its meat (called mutton or lamb) or its wool
words.exampleEn: a flock of sheep

words.definitionEn: an area of sand or small stones (called shingle), next to the sea or a lake
words.exampleEn: on the beach, tourists sunbathing on the beach

words.definitionEn: a piece of clothing worn on the upper part of the body, made of light cloth, with sleeves and usually with a collar and buttons down the front
words.exampleEn: to wear a shirt

words.definitionEn: one of a pair of objects usually made of leather or plastic that you wear on your feet
words.exampleEn: a pair of shoes

words.definitionEn: a building or part of a building where you can buy goods or services
words.exampleEn: to open/close/run a shop

words.definitionEn: to buy things in shops
words.exampleEn: I shop there from time to time.

words.definitionEn: the activity of going to shops and buying things or ordering them online
words.exampleEn: to go shopping

words.definitionEn: measuring or covering a small length or distance, or a smaller length or distance than usual
words.exampleEn: He had short curly hair.

words.definitionEn: used to show what is right, appropriate, etc., especially when criticizing somebody’s actions
words.exampleEn: You shouldn't drink and drive.

words.definitionEn: a programme on television or the radio
words.exampleEn: Millions of people watch the show.

words.definitionEn: to make something clear; to prove something
words.exampleEn: show (that)…, The figures clearly show that her claims are false.

words.definitionEn: a piece of equipment producing a flow of water that you stand under to wash yourself; the small room or part of a room that contains a shower
words.exampleEn: in the shower, He's in the shower.

words.definitionEn: physically or mentally ill
words.exampleEn: a sick child

words.definitionEn: like somebody/something but not exactly the same
words.exampleEn: We have very similar interests.

words.definitionEn: to make musical sounds with your voice in the form of a song or tune
words.exampleEn: I just can't sing in tune!

words.definitionEn: a person who sings, or whose job is singing, especially in public
words.exampleEn: She's a wonderful singer.

words.definitionEn: having beauty; giving pleasure to the senses or to the mind
words.exampleEn: a beautiful woman/girl

words.definitionEn: a girl or woman who has the same mother and father as another person
words.exampleEn: She's my sister.

words.definitionEn: to rest your weight on your bottom with your back straight, for example on/in a chair
words.exampleEn: She sat and stared at the letter in front of her.

words.definitionEn: all the circumstances and things that are happening at a particular time and in a particular place
words.exampleEn: the present economic/financial/political situation
There are six cookies left.

words.definitionEn: the ability to do something well
words.exampleEn: The job requires skill and an eye for detail.

words.definitionEn: a piece of clothing that hangs from the middle part of the body
words.exampleEn: a short/long/pleated/denim skirt

words.definitionEn: for the reason that
words.exampleEn: I did it because he told me to.

words.definitionEn: to rest with your eyes closed and your mind and body not active
words.exampleEn: Let her sleep—it'll do her good.

words.definitionEn: to start to be something
words.exampleEn: + adj., It soon became apparent that no one was going to come.

words.definitionEn: not moving, acting or done quickly; taking a long time; not fast
words.exampleEn: a slow pace/speed

words.definitionEn: a piece of furniture for sleeping on
words.exampleEn: a single/double bed

words.definitionEn: not large in size, number, degree, amount, etc.
words.exampleEn: a small town/village/community

words.definitionEn: a room for sleeping in
words.exampleEn: the spare/guest bedroom

words.definitionEn: a reptile with a very long thin body and no legs. There are many types of snake, some of which are poisonous.
words.exampleEn: a snake coiled up in the grass

words.definitionEn: small soft white pieces (called flakes) of frozen water that fall from the sky in cold weather; this substance when it is lying on the ground
words.exampleEn: Snow was falling heavily.

words.definitionEn: when it snows, snow falls from the sky
words.exampleEn: It's been snowing heavily all day.

words.definitionEn: to such a great degree
words.exampleEn: Don't look so angry.

words.definitionEn: used to show the reason for something
words.exampleEn: It was still painful, so I went to see a doctor.

words.definitionEn: an alcoholic drink made from malt with hops added to give it taste. There are many types of beer.
words.exampleEn: a pint/can/bottle of beer

words.definitionEn: used with uncountable nouns or plural countable nouns to mean ‘an amount of’ or ‘a number of’, when the amount or number is not given
words.exampleEn: There's some milk in the fridge.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to an amount of something or a number of people or things when the amount or number is not given
words.exampleEn: Some disapprove of the idea.

words.definitionEn: a person who is not known or mentioned by name
words.exampleEn: Somebody should have told me.

words.definitionEn: a person who is not known or mentioned by name
words.exampleEn: There's someone at the door.

words.definitionEn: a thing that is not known or mentioned by name
words.exampleEn: We stopped for something to eat.

words.definitionEn: occasionally rather than all of the time
words.exampleEn: Sometimes I go by car.

words.definitionEn: a person’s male child
words.exampleEn: their four-year-old son

words.definitionEn: a short piece of music with words that you sing
words.exampleEn: a love/pop/rock song

words.definitionEn: in a short time from now; a short time after something else has happened
words.exampleEn: She sold the house soon after her husband died.

words.definitionEn: feeling sad and ashamed about something that has been done
words.exampleEn: sorry about something, We're very sorry about the damage to your car.

words.definitionEn: used when you are apologizing for something
words.exampleEn: Sorry I'm late!

words.definitionEn: something that you can hear
words.exampleEn: a loud/soft/faint sound

words.definitionEn: to give a particular impression when heard or read about
words.exampleEn: + adj., to sound good/great/right/interesting

words.definitionEn: a liquid food made by boiling meat, vegetables, etc. in water, often eaten as the first course of a meal
words.exampleEn: a bowl/cup of soup

words.definitionEn: in or towards the south
words.exampleEn: South Wales

words.definitionEn: earlier than somebody/something
words.exampleEn: before lunch

words.definitionEn: towards the south
words.exampleEn: This room faces south.

words.definitionEn: the direction that is on your right when you watch the sun rise; one of the four main points of the compass
words.exampleEn: Which way is south?

words.definitionEn: the area outside the earth’s atmosphere where all the other planets and stars are
words.exampleEn: in space, Valentina Tereshkova was the first woman in space.

words.definitionEn: to talk to somebody about something; to have a conversation with somebody
words.exampleEn: speak to somebody, The President refused to speak to reporters.

words.definitionEn: not ordinary or usual; different from what is normal
words.exampleEn: The school will only allow this in special circumstances.

words.definitionEn: to start doing something; to do the first part of something
words.exampleEn: Shall I begin?

words.definitionEn: the time when something starts; the first part of an event, a story, etc.
words.exampleEn: A story has to have a beginning, middle, and end.

words.definitionEn: to say or write the letters of a word in the correct order
words.exampleEn: How do you spell your surname?

words.definitionEn: the act of forming words correctly from individual letters; the ability to do this
words.exampleEn: the differences between British and American spelling

words.definitionEn: to give money to pay for goods, services, etc.
words.exampleEn: spend something, I've spent all my money already.

words.definitionEn: activity that you do for pleasure and that needs physical effort or skill, usually done in a special area and according to fixed rules
words.exampleEn: There are excellent facilities for sport and recreation.

words.definitionEn: the season between winter and summer when plants begin to grow
words.exampleEn: The following spring, the three artists travelled to California.

words.definitionEn: at or towards the back of somebody/something; further back
words.exampleEn: She rode off down the road with the dog running behind.

words.definitionEn: at or towards the back of somebody/something, and often hidden by it or them
words.exampleEn: Who's the girl standing behind Jan?

words.definitionEn: to be on your feet; to be in a vertical position
words.exampleEn: She was too weak to stand.

words.definitionEn: a large ball of burning gas in space that we see as a point of light in the sky at night
words.exampleEn: There was a big moon and hundreds of stars were shining overhead.

words.definitionEn: to begin doing or using something
words.exampleEn: start (something), I start work at nine.

words.definitionEn: something that you say or write that gives information or an opinion
words.exampleEn: Are the following statements true or false?

words.definitionEn: a place where trains stop so that passengers can get on and off; the buildings connected with this
words.exampleEn: the main station

words.definitionEn: to feel certain that something is true or that somebody is telling you the truth
words.exampleEn: believe somebody, I don't believe you!

words.definitionEn: to continue to be in a particular place for a period of time without moving away
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., Stay there and don't move!

words.definitionEn: continuing until a particular point in time and not finishing
words.exampleEn: I am very happy that you all are still alive and well.

words.definitionEn: a place where a bus or train stops regularly for passengers to get on or off
words.exampleEn: I get off at the next stop.

words.definitionEn: to no longer move; to make somebody/something no longer move
words.exampleEn: The car stopped at the traffic lights.

words.definitionEn: a description of events and people that the writer or speaker has invented in order to entertain people
words.exampleEn: a tragic love story

words.definitionEn: at or to a lower level, position or place
words.exampleEn: They live on the floor below.

words.definitionEn: a public road in a city or town that has houses and buildings on one side or both sides
words.exampleEn: along/down/up the street, I was just walking along the street when it happened.

words.definitionEn: at or to a lower level or position than somebody/something
words.exampleEn: He dived below the surface of the water.

words.definitionEn: having a lot of physical power so that you can lift heavy weights, do hard physical work, etc.
words.exampleEn: He's strong enough to lift a car!

words.definitionEn: a person who is studying at a university or college
words.exampleEn: a college/university student

words.definitionEn: the activity of learning or gaining knowledge, either from books or by examining things in the world
words.exampleEn: a room set aside for private study

words.definitionEn: to spend time learning about a subject by reading, going to college, etc.
words.exampleEn: He sat up very late that night, studying.

words.definitionEn: the particular way in which something is done
words.exampleEn: a wide range of musical styles

words.definitionEn: a thing or person that is being discussed, described or dealt with
words.exampleEn: subject of something, Walker's work has been the subject of much debate.

words.definitionEn: the fact that you have achieved something that you want and have been trying to do or get
words.exampleEn: The company has achieved considerable success in this market.

words.definitionEn: a sweet substance, often in the form of white or brown crystals, made from the juices of various plants, used in cooking or to make tea, coffee, etc. sweeter
words.exampleEn: Do you take sugar (= have it in your tea, coffee, etc.)?

words.definitionEn: the warmest season of the year, coming between spring and autumn
words.exampleEn: in the summer, We're going away in the summer.

words.definitionEn: the star that shines in the sky during the day and gives the earth heat and light
words.exampleEn: The sun was shining and birds were singing.

words.definitionEn: the day of the week after Saturday and before Monday, thought of as either the first or the last day of the week
words.exampleEn: It's Sunday today, isn't it?

words.definitionEn: a large shop that sells food, drinks and goods used in the home. People choose what they want from the shelves and pay for it as they leave.
words.exampleEn: I only shop at this supermarket.

words.definitionEn: confident that you know something or that you are right
words.exampleEn: ‘Is that John over there?’ ‘I'm not sure’.

words.definitionEn: a piece of clothing for the upper part of the body, made of wool or cotton, with long sleeves
words.exampleEn: She wore jeans and a sweater.

words.definitionEn: to move through water by moving your arms and legs, without touching the bottom
words.exampleEn: I can't swim.

words.definitionEn: the sport or activity of swimming
words.exampleEn: Swimming is a good form of exercise.

words.definitionEn: of the most excellent type or quality
words.exampleEn: That's the best movie I've ever seen!

words.definitionEn: an informal shirt, usually with short sleeves (= arms) no collar or buttons, or just a few buttons at the top
words.exampleEn: He was wearing a faded pair of blue jeans and an old T-shirt.

words.definitionEn: a piece of furniture that consists of a flat top supported by legs
words.exampleEn: at a/the table, We sat at a round table in the corner.

words.definitionEn: to carry or move something from one place to another
words.exampleEn: take something, Remember to take your coat when you leave.

words.definitionEn: to say things; to speak in order to give information or to express feelings, ideas, etc.
words.exampleEn: Stop talking and listen!

words.definitionEn: having a greater than average height
words.exampleEn: She's tall and thin.

words.definitionEn: a car with a driver that you pay to take you somewhere. Taxis usually have meters that show how much money you have to pay.
words.exampleEn: We took a taxi to the airport.

words.definitionEn: the dried leaves (called tea leaves) of the tea bush
words.exampleEn: a packet of tea

words.definitionEn: to give lessons to students in a school, college, university, etc.; to help somebody learn something by giving information about it
words.exampleEn: She teaches at our local school.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is teaching, especially in a school
words.exampleEn: an English/a science teacher

words.definitionEn: a group of people who play a particular game or sport against another group of people
words.exampleEn: a football/basketball/soccer team

words.definitionEn: a person who is between 13 and 19 years old
words.exampleEn: a magazine aimed at teenagers

words.definitionEn: a system for talking to somebody else over long distances, using wires or radio; a machine used for this
words.exampleEn: The telephone rang and Pat answered it.

words.definitionEn: to speak to somebody by phone
words.exampleEn: Please write or telephone for details.

words.definitionEn: a piece of electrical equipment with a screen on which you can watch programmes with moving pictures and sounds
words.exampleEn: a widescreen/flat-screen/plasma television

words.definitionEn: to give information to somebody by speaking or writing
words.exampleEn: tell something to somebody, He told the news to everybody he saw.
There are only ten of these rare animals left.

words.definitionEn: of a higher standard or less poor quality; not as bad as something else
words.exampleEn: We're hoping for better weather tomorrow.

words.definitionEn: a game in which two or four players use rackets to hit a ball backwards and forwards across a net on a specially marked court
words.exampleEn: to play tennis

words.definitionEn: very unpleasant; making you feel very unhappy, upset or frightened
words.exampleEn: a terrible experience

words.definitionEn: an examination of somebody’s knowledge or ability, consisting of questions for them to answer or activities for them to perform
words.exampleEn: an IQ/a fitness test

words.definitionEn: to find out how much somebody knows, or what they can do by asking them questions or giving them activities to perform
words.exampleEn: test somebody, Students will be tested at the end of each module.

words.definitionEn: any form of written material
words.exampleEn: a computer that can process text

words.definitionEn: used to introduce the second part of a comparison
words.exampleEn: I'm older than her.

words.definitionEn: used to introduce the second part of a comparison
words.exampleEn: I'm older than her.

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody that you are grateful for something
words.exampleEn: thank somebody for something, I must write and thank Mary for the present.
